findings.evidence: `rg -n "elementaryTwoQuotientMap_comp|\@\[simp\].*elementaryTwoQuotientMap_comp" code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean` shows the theorem at line 209 with no `@[simp]` annotation.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `elementaryTwoQuotientMap_comp` if this orientation is intended as the normal form.
findings.issue: `elementaryTwoQuotientMap_comp` is a characteristic normal-form lemma for induced maps but is not marked `@[simp]`, so `simp` will not reduce `elementaryTwoQuotientMap (g.comp f) x` for arbitrary quotient elements.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "elementaryTwoQuotientCongr_trans|\@\[simp\].*elementaryTwoQuotientCongr_trans" code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean` shows the theorem at line 246 with no `@[simp]` annotation.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `elementaryTwoQuotientCongr_trans` in the generic API.
findings.issue: `elementaryTwoQuotientCongr_trans` is the equivalence-chain normal form but is not marked `@[simp]`, blocking automated normalization of transported class chains.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "elementaryTwoQuotientCongr_trans|\@\[simp\].*elementaryTwoQuotientCongr_trans" code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/ClassGroup/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean` shows the wrapper at line 146 with no `@[simp]` annotation.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to the class-group wrapper as well.
findings.issue: The class-group wrapper `elementaryTwoQuotientCongr_trans` repeats the same unannotated composition normal form, so genus-field code using the specialized names still cannot rely on `simp`.

summary: The transport API now exposes the needed functoriality lemmas, but the composition normal forms are not annotated for automation, so downstream chains of induced maps/equivalences will not normalize by `simp` on arbitrary quotient elements.
